message: Good morning, Boss. About today's schedule,
Boss, shouldn't you be a bit more aware of the crisis?
Just having a sense of crisis doesn’t really change anything.
Well, I'm getting my salary on time, so I won't complain.
Sometimes I just want to do a job that really feels like 'this is what being a private detective is all about!'.
Did I stray off the path?
A client here for a request!?
office. It's a small and messy place, but I consider it my little paradise.
It’s not an exaggeration to say it's everything in my current life.
The office may feel a bit too simple, but it's a cozy space for me.
Once I'm able to live independently as a detective,
I'll think about moving to a fancy office.
My secretary Yuriko is a bit clumsy and scatterbrained, but she's bright and never boring to watch.
W-well, the stuff I'm wearing is just cheap!
You didn't say anything like that.
No, I mean, the boss's clothes are brand-name, and there's a huge price gap with my common clothes!
Do you think I have holes in my eyes or something?
Sure, I get my salary properly and even got a raise, but I haven't had decent income in a year.
But still, the boss's outfits are
always brand-name stuff. I can tell at least that much.
That's just a matter of liking fashion.
You can't keep buying clothes that cost over a thousand bucks just for fun, right?!
C'mon, let's stop chatting and get to work!
I think it's easy to get down when you're stuck in a situation like a deadweight, but Yuriko is
always full of energy without a care in the world.
Feels more like we're just tea-drinking buddies, huh?
Our job just feels like we're
barely going through the motions, doesn't it? Like we're just here for tea.
You know, I actually wanted to be a detective.
That's why you came to us, right?
I’ve been feeling kind of down since this morning.
So, what job are we doing today?
I'm so bored that I might study crime
It's great to be studious, Yuriko. By the way,
there was that serial murder case.
Modern America is sick. Men who can't even talk to women normally turn to perverse sex crimes. It's unforgivable!
Aren't you going to stop sighing as part of your daily routine?
I'd like to if I could, but you know...
It's tough when we don't get any work every day.
Once again, he lets out a sigh.
I want to work too. But if we don't get any requests...
That's it! Boss, let's do some publicity!
But don't you wonder if that will really make a huge difference?
I don't think it can get any worse than now!
Yuriko, you might have forgotten already, but...
Hey, remember when we distributed flyers but they had no effect?
I have a memory of prank calls coming in nonstop, and Yuriko was also fed up with handling them.
W-well, the past is the past.
I, for one, dream of a scenario where a beautiful client knocks on this door.
Please awaken to your reality as a business owner.
Yuriko, I'm not a businessman, I'm a private detective. Fortunately, I’m not struggling for money.
It was a mistake to talk seriously to the Boss. Well, whatever. I was foolish to have expectations.
Ah, I wonder if any job will come, no matter what it is.
Why did you think of becoming a detective?
Well, it’s just because female detectives are cool.
Are they really that cool, though?
Isn't it tough in reality? I mean, there's stakeouts and shootouts and all that.
Boss, you’ve read too many detective novels.
There are plenty of female detectives in this Manhattan.
And most of them are dealing with cases like infidelity investigations, huh?
Not at all! There must be a lot of people chasing murderers!
In the real world, chasing murderers is the police's job.
Instead of teasing me, let’s focus on work.
It felt kind of like a dream. Even though my target wasn’t there, it was a fun party.
Calling it a dream feels kind of exaggerated.
Exaggerated or not, it’s an experience that you can’t have just once in a lifetime!
There are people who can't even experience something like that once in a lifetime.
There are a lot of them, aren't there!
In my life, it was truly a moment from a dream.
I wonder if it was worth inviting after all.
I'm thankful to the Boss. But,
a troubling fact has emerged that I can't just celebrate about.
Hey Boss, what should we do?
What do you mean, what happened?
There's no evidence that I was even there.
At this rate, they'll think I'm lying if I brag to my friends.
Speaking of which, we didn't take any photos.
Oh gosh, it really might have been a dream.
If I get another chance, I'll definitely take pictures next time!
By the way, Boss, didn't you say that the topic of the serial murder case came up at the party?
Yeah, that's right. Apparently, there were people who thought Jennifer might be the culprit.
Jennifer Rumford!? Where did that story even come from? It's absurd.
No, I think it was something about her having done something murder-like in the past or something along those lines.
Huh? The Boss isn't taking it seriously.
Yeah. I mean, it was Tonny who said it. I wonder why he would say something like that.
It's true, it's just too cruel, no matter what.
To just say he's the culprit.
But what does the culprit think when they take parts of the victim's body?
I think there's some significance to that action, though.
For a collector, the places being cut are all over the place.
No way, are they trying to create an android with that or something?
In that case, wouldn't it be better not to slice up and collect anything?
Since there are no requests, shall we think about what a serial killer might look like?
I originally started this detective agency as a hobby, but I never imagined it would be so quiet.
I've learned the hard way that life isn't so sweet.
According to Yuriko, we should do more publicity.
At least, that’s what she says.
Ideally, clients would come in quietly after hearing about this office through word of mouth.
I've been hearing Yuriko's morning sighs almost every day for the past year.
But it’s not my fault. It's those who don’t hire a talented guy like me.
I’m technically a secretary, but I’m mostly just given all the odd jobs, so it feels like I’m just a general errand runner.
Still, coming here like this makes me wonder if it's because I’m genuinely serious or if I just want to be in a job with 'detective' in the title.
Which one is it, I wonder?
Anyway, I've been greatly helped by her.
I think if we keep going like this, we'll become good partners.
I hope Yuriko can become a bit more capable.
I wonder if that's being selfish.
The guest lineup and the food were top-notch.
I didn't even have time to chat with Guy, but it was amazing to talk to someone I usually only see on screen or TV.
It was a glamorous party after a long time. I probably won't see such a luxurious cast again.
I guess I'm being a bit of a fanboy myself.
However, I enjoyed it thanks to Guy's invitation.
Still, it was kind of a dull party.
Instead of simply enjoying the party, the exhausting conversations about probing each other's motives were tiring.
I wonder if there's a party out there that's just more fun.
It all started when a girl was murdered around Los Angeles, and since then, three victims have come up with the same M.O.
The characteristic is that each girl was missing a part of their body.
Also, all three were dismembered alive and died from blood loss.
The crime scenes spread across Los Angeles, San Francisco, and Detroit.
It's hard to imagine what kind of person the culprit is, but I believe this was all done by just one insane individual.
Lately, I think it's become an unpleasant world with too many messed-up guys.
